title: Expansion of unsized single bit value not working in port map
body:
Unsized single bit values preceded by an apostrophe expand to fill the entire width of the target vector in system verilog

e.g. `logic [1:0] foo = '1; // Equivalent to = 2'b11`

Yosys appears to incorrectly only assign the lowest bit in the vector, when this style of assignment is used in a port map.

N.B. I assume this is related to #2570 , but I don't really know anything about how the yosys verilog frontend works, so can't provide an opinion!

## Steps to reproduce the issue

Save the following source as broken.sv:
```
module hier
(
    input [1:0] in,
    output [1:0] out,
);
    assign out = in;
endmodule

module broken
(
    output [1:0] out
);

    hier inst
    (
        .in('1),
        .out(out)
    );
endmodule
```
Run `yosys -p "read_verilog -sv broken.sv; synth_ice40; write_verilog"`

Observe the value assigned to `out`

I am running the following build of yosys:
`Yosys 0.9+3981 (git sha1 0e0f8429, clang 11.0.0 -fPIC -Os)`

## Expected behavior

`out` is assigned 2'h3

## Actual behavior

`out` is assigned 2'h1
